epics.title: Migrate Jupyter Notebook to Plugin
epics.description: Extract the Jupyter notebook viewer/editor from internal/notebook/ into the numentext-jupyter plugin repo. Convert from a compiled-in Go package to a Lua plugin using the plugin system's file type handler and panel APIs. The core NumenText binary should have no Jupyter code after migration.

user_stories.title: Scaffold numentext-jupyter plugin repo
user_stories.description: Initialize the numentext-jupyter GitHub repo with plugin.json manifest, init.lua entry point, LICENSE (Apache 2.0), README, and basic directory structure. The manifest should claim .ipynb file type and register relevant commands and shortcuts.
acceptance_criteria: Given the numentext-jupyter repo, when cloned to ~/.numentext/plugins/jupyter/, then NumenText discovers it on startup
Given plugin.json, when parsed, then it declares file_types [".ipynb"], name "jupyter", and version
Given the repo, when checked, then it contains LICENSE (Apache 2.0) matching the core project
Given the README, when read, then it explains installation via --plugin-install and usage

user_stories.title: Port .ipynb parser to Lua
user_stories.description: Rewrite the notebook JSON parser (internal/notebook/parser.go) as a Lua module. Parse .ipynb v4 format into cells with type, source, outputs, and metadata. Use Lua's built-in JSON decoding or a lightweight JSON library.
acceptance_criteria: Given a valid .ipynb v4 file, when parsed by the Lua module, then all cells are extracted with correct type (code/markdown/raw)
Given a code cell with outputs, when parsed, then stdout text and error tracebacks are extracted
Given a cell with base64 image output, when parsed, then the image data is available for saving
Given notebook metadata with kernelspec, when parsed, then the kernel language is extracted
Given an invalid or corrupt .ipynb file, when parsed, then a clear error is returned without crashing

user_stories.title: Port cell rendering and navigation to Lua plugin
user_stories.description: Rewrite the NotebookView cell rendering and navigation using the plugin panel API. Register a file type handler for .ipynb that renders cells in a plugin panel. Support navigation mode (Up/Down between cells) and edit mode (Enter to edit cell content).
acceptance_criteria: Given a .ipynb file opened, when the plugin handles it, then cells render in the panel with syntax highlighting for code and styled text for markdown
Given navigation mode, when Up/Down is pressed, then the selected cell changes
Given a selected cell, when Enter is pressed, then the cell enters edit mode
Given edit mode, when Escape is pressed, then the cell returns to navigation mode
Given navigation mode, when 'a' or 'b' is pressed, then a new cell is inserted above or below
Given navigation mode, when 'dd' is pressed, then the current cell is deleted

user_stories.title: Port cell execution to Lua plugin
user_stories.description: Rewrite cell execution (Python subprocess or Jupyter REST API) as Lua code using the plugin host API for process execution. Support Shift+Enter to execute and advance, Ctrl+Shift+Enter to run all cells.
acceptance_criteria: Given a code cell, when Shift+Enter is pressed, then the cell code executes via Python subprocess and output appears below the cell
Given execution output with stdout, when displayed, then text output appears below the cell
Given execution output with an error, when displayed, then the traceback appears in red below the cell
Given Ctrl+Shift+Enter, when pressed, then all code cells execute in order from top to bottom
Given no Python installed, when execution is attempted, then a clear error message is shown

user_stories.title: Remove Jupyter code from core and verify plugin works
user_stories.description: Delete internal/notebook/ from the core NumenText repo. Remove all notebook references from app.go. Verify that installing the plugin via --plugin-install restores full .ipynb functionality. Update README to document the plugin.
acceptance_criteria: Given the core NumenText binary, when built without the plugin, then no notebook code is included and binary size decreases
Given the core binary, when opening a .ipynb file without the plugin, then it opens as plain text (JSON)
Given 'numentext --plugin-install github.com/numentech-co/numentext-jupyter', when executed, then the plugin installs
Given the plugin installed, when opening a .ipynb file, then the notebook viewer renders cells correctly
Given the core README, when read, then Jupyter is listed as an optional plugin with install instructions

epics.title: Migrate Git Tools to Plugin
epics.description: Extract git diff display, gutter markers, diff view dialog, and the three-pane merge tool from the core into the numentext-git plugin repo. The plugin uses the event listener and panel APIs to provide git integration as an installable extension.

user_stories.title: Scaffold numentext-git plugin repo
user_stories.description: Initialize the numentext-git GitHub repo with plugin.json, init.lua, LICENSE, README, and directory structure. Register commands for git diff, merge view, and git blame. Register event listeners for file open/save to refresh diff markers.
acceptance_criteria: Given the numentext-git repo, when cloned to ~/.numentext/plugins/git/, then NumenText discovers it on startup
Given plugin.json, when parsed, then it declares commands for git-diff, git-merge, git-blame
Given the repo, when checked, then it contains LICENSE (Apache 2.0) and README with install instructions
Given the plugin loaded, when the View menu opens, then Git Diff and Merge View items appear

user_stories.title: Port git diff gutter markers to Lua plugin
user_stories.description: Rewrite the git diff marker computation (internal/editor/gitdiff.go) as a Lua module. Use numen.on('file_open') and numen.on('file_save') events to trigger diff computation. Use a host API extension to set gutter markers on specific lines.
acceptance_criteria: Given a file in a git repo with changes, when opened with the plugin active, then green/red/blue gutter markers appear for added/deleted/modified lines
Given a file saved, when the save event fires, then diff markers refresh automatically
Given a file not in a git repo, when opened, then no markers appear and no errors occur
Given git is not installed, when a file opens, then no markers appear and no errors occur

user_stories.title: Port diff view dialog to Lua plugin
user_stories.description: Rewrite the unified diff dialog (GitDiffDialog in dialog.go) as a plugin command. The plugin registers a 'Git Diff' command that shows the diff output in a plugin panel with color-coded lines.
acceptance_criteria: Given the Git Diff command, when invoked from View menu or command palette, then the diff output appears in a panel
Given diff output, when rendered, then added lines show in green, removed in red, context in default
Given an unmodified file, when diff is requested, then the panel shows 'No changes'
Given the diff panel, when Escape is pressed, then it closes

user_stories.title: Port three-pane merge tool to plugin
user_stories.description: Rewrite the merge view (internal/mergeview/) as a plugin component. This is the most complex migration since it requires a custom multi-pane UI. The plugin registers a file type handler for conflicted files and a --merge CLI integration.
acceptance_criteria: Given a file with conflict markers, when 'Merge View' is selected, then the three-pane view opens with LOCAL/BASE/REMOTE + RESULT
Given the merge view, when conflict resolution shortcuts are used (1/2/3/a), then conflicts are resolved correctly
Given all conflicts resolved, when Ctrl+S is pressed, then the result is saved
Given 'numentext --merge LOCAL BASE REMOTE MERGED' with the plugin installed, then the merge view opens

user_stories.title: Remove git code from core and verify plugin works
user_stories.description: Delete internal/editor/gitdiff.go, internal/mergeview/, and git-related code from app.go and dialog.go. Verify plugin restores functionality. Keep the --merge CLI flag in main.go but delegate to the plugin if installed.
acceptance_criteria: Given the core binary without the plugin, when built, then no git diff or merge code is included
Given the core binary, when View > Git Diff is selected without the plugin, then nothing happens or a message says 'Install numentext-git plugin'
Given the plugin installed, when git diff features are used, then they work identically to the built-in version
Given the core README, when read, then git integration is listed as an optional plugin

epics.title: Migrate HTML Tools to Plugin
epics.description: Extract HTML encode/decode and entity picker from the core into the numentext-html plugin repo. Simple migration since the feature is self-contained with no event hooks or file type claims.

user_stories.title: Scaffold numentext-html plugin repo
user_stories.description: Initialize the numentext-html GitHub repo with plugin.json, init.lua, LICENSE, README. Register three commands: html-encode, html-decode, insert-html-entity. Register Edit menu items for each.
acceptance_criteria: Given the numentext-html repo, when cloned to ~/.numentext/plugins/html/, then NumenText discovers it on startup
Given plugin.json, when parsed, then it declares three commands for encode, decode, and entity picker
Given the plugin loaded, when Edit menu opens, then HTML Encode, HTML Decode, and Insert HTML Entity items appear

user_stories.title: Port HTML encode/decode to Lua plugin
user_stories.description: Rewrite HTML entity encoding/decoding (internal/editor/html.go) as Lua functions. Use numen.active_content() to get text, numen.insert_text() to replace. Support selection-only operation via cursor/selection APIs.
acceptance_criteria: Given selected text '<div>', when HTML Encode command runs, then it becomes '<div>'
Given selected text '&', when HTML Decode command runs, then it becomes '&'
Given no selection, when encode runs, then the entire file content is encoded
Given the operation completes, when Ctrl+Z is pressed, then the change is undone

user_stories.title: Port HTML entity picker to Lua plugin
user_stories.description: Rewrite the entity picker dialog (html_entity_dialog.go) as a Lua-driven dialog. Use the plugin panel API to show a searchable list of entities. Selecting an entity inserts it at the cursor.
acceptance_criteria: Given the Insert HTML Entity command, when invoked, then a panel shows a list of common entities
Given the entity list, when the user types 'space', then only   and similar are shown
Given an entity is selected, when confirmed, then the entity code is inserted at the cursor
Given the picker, when Escape is pressed, then it closes without inserting

user_stories.title: Remove HTML code from core and verify plugin works
user_stories.description: Delete internal/editor/html.go, internal/ui/html_entity_dialog.go, and HTML menu items from app.go. Verify plugin restores functionality.
acceptance_criteria: Given the core binary without the plugin, when built, then no HTML tool code is included
Given the core binary, when Edit menu opens without the plugin, then HTML items are absent
Given the plugin installed, when HTML tools are used, then they work identically
Given the core README, when read, then HTML tools are listed as an optional plugin

epics.title: Migrate TODO Scanner to Plugin
epics.description: Extract the TODO/FIXME/BUG annotation scanner and annotations panel from the core into the numentext-todo plugin repo. The plugin uses event listeners to scan on file save and a panel to display results.

user_stories.title: Scaffold numentext-todo plugin repo
user_stories.description: Initialize the numentext-todo GitHub repo with plugin.json, init.lua, LICENSE, README. Register the annotations panel, Ctrl+Shift+A shortcut, and file_save event listener.
acceptance_criteria: Given the numentext-todo repo, when cloned to ~/.numentext/plugins/todo/, then NumenText discovers it on startup
Given plugin.json, when parsed, then it declares a panel, a shortcut (Ctrl+Shift+A), and a Tools menu item
Given the plugin loaded, when Tools menu opens, then 'Annotations' item appears

user_stories.title: Port annotation scanner to Lua plugin
user_stories.description: Rewrite the comment scanning logic (internal/editor/annotations.go) as a Lua module. Detect TODO, FIXME, BUG, HACK, WONTFIX, XXX, NOTE in comments. Use numen.on('file_save') to trigger re-scan. Use numen.active_content() to get file text.
acceptance_criteria: Given a file with '// TODO: fix this', when scanned, then the annotation is detected with tag=TODO and text='fix this'
Given a file with '# FIXME: broken' (Python), when scanned, then the annotation is detected
Given a file saved, when the save event fires, then annotations for that file are re-scanned
Given TODO inside a string literal, when scanned, then it is ignored (best effort)

user_stories.title: Port annotations panel to Lua plugin
user_stories.description: Rewrite the annotations panel (internal/editor/annotations_panel.go) using the plugin panel API. Show all annotations grouped by file with line numbers. Clicking jumps to location.
acceptance_criteria: Given annotations in multiple files, when the panel opens, then all annotations are listed sorted by file then line
Given an annotation entry, when displayed, then it shows filename:line tag and description text
Given an annotation entry, when selected, then the editor opens that file and jumps to the line
Given Ctrl+Shift+A, when pressed, then the annotations panel toggles open/closed

user_stories.title: Remove TODO scanner from core and verify plugin works
user_stories.description: Delete internal/editor/annotations.go, annotations_panel.go, and related code from app.go. Verify plugin restores functionality.
acceptance_criteria: Given the core binary without the plugin, when built, then no annotation scanner code is included
Given the core binary, when Ctrl+Shift+A is pressed without the plugin, then nothing happens
Given the plugin installed, when annotations features are used, then they work identically
Given the core README, when read, then TODO scanner is listed as an optional plugin
